mnemonics: S.E.R.V.L.E.T. - Server Extension Running Virtually Locally Executing Tasks
keyPoints: Servlets are Java classes that extend server capabilities and handle client requests.
They run inside a servlet container (like Tomcat) which manages their lifecycle.
The core methods are init(), service(), doGet(), doPost(), and destroy().
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call super() when overriding service(), doGet(), or doPost().
Not properly handling multithreading issues, as servlets are shared by multiple requests.
